quality.rationale: 涉及弯曲时空波动方程在乌龟坐标下的渐近行为推导，逻辑链条完整且适用于各类旋转黑洞微扰场景

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s17_17.16_c.md]Problem 17.16 (c) Find the asymptotic form of $\Phi$ for $\mathbf{r} \to \inf ty$ .## Solution(c) It is convenient (but not essential) to introduce a “tortoise” coordinate $\mathbf{r}^*$ which satisfies $\mathrm{dr}^* / \mathrm{dr} = (\mathbf{r}^2 + \mathbf{a}^2) / \Delta$ . Note that the interval $(\mathbf{r}_+, \inf ty)$ in the r-coordinate is stretched to $(- \inf ty, \inf ty)$ in $\mathbf{r}^*$ . Equation (4) now becomes$$\frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathrm {R}}{\mathrm {d r}^{* 2}} + \frac {2 \mathrm {r} \Delta}{\left(\mathrm {r}^{2} + \mathrm {a}^{2}\right)^{2}} \frac {\mathrm {d R}}{\mathrm {d r}^{*}} + \left[ \omega^{2} + \frac {\mathrm {a}^{2} \mathrm {m}^{2} - 4 \operatorname {M a r m} \omega - \Delta \mathrm {A}}{\left(\mathrm {r}^{2} + \mathrm {a}^{2}\right)^{2}}\right] \mathrm {R} = 0. \tag {5}$$As $\mathbf{r} \to \inf ty$ , we get$$\frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathrm {R}}{\mathrm {d r}^{* 2}} + \frac {2}{\mathrm {r}} \frac {\mathrm {d R}}{\mathrm {d r}^{*}} + \omega^{2} \mathrm {R} \approx 0$$which has solutions$$R \sim r^{- 1} e^{\pm i \omega r^{*}}$$corresponding to ingoing and outgoing waves.
origin.solution: (c) It is convenient (but not essential) to introduce a “tortoise” coordinate $\mathbf{r}^*$ which satisfies $\mathrm{dr}^* / \mathrm{dr} = (\mathbf{r}^2 + \mathbf{a}^2) / \Delta$ . Note that the interval $(\mathbf{r}_+, \inf ty)$ in the r-coordinate is stretched to $(- \inf ty, \inf ty)$ in $\mathbf{r}^*$ . Equation (4) now becomes$$\frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathrm {R}}{\mathrm {d r}^{* 2}} + \frac {2 \mathrm {r} \Delta}{\left(\mathrm {r}^{2} + \mathrm {a}^{2}\right)^{2}} \frac {\mathrm {d R}}{\mathrm {d r}^{*}} + \left[ \omega^{2} + \frac {\mathrm {a}^{2} \mathrm {m}^{2} - 4 \operatorname {M a r m} \omega - \Delta \mathrm {A}}{\left(\mathrm {r}^{2} + \mathrm {a}^{2}\right)^{2}}\right] \mathrm {R} = 0. \tag {5}$$As $\mathbf{r} \to \inf ty$ , we get$$\frac {\mathrm {d}^{2} \mathrm {R}}{\mathrm {d r}^{* 2}} + \frac {2}{\mathrm {r}} \frac {\mathrm {d R}}{\mathrm {d r}^{*}} + \omega^{2} \mathrm {R} \approx 0$$which has solutions$$R \sim r^{- 1} e^{\pm i \omega r^{*}}$$corresponding to ingoing and outgoing waves.
